title: Flow If/Else (LLM)
description: A two-branch conditional router whose decision is made by an LLM.
Instead of a Python expression, the user supplies a natural-language
yes/no question (e.g. 'Is there a dog in this image?', 'Is this an
invoice?'). For every chunk, the node sends the chunk plus the
question to a wired LLM via the control channel and routes the chunk
to the THEN branch on YES or the ELSE branch on NO. The invoke
channel is provider-agnostic — connect any llm_* (Claude, OpenAI,
Mistral, Gemini, Ollama). For image lanes, connect a vision-capable
LLM. Audio and video lanes need preprocessing (frame grabber, STT)
upstream because LLM APIs do not accept raw media buffers.

llm.description: LLM that evaluates the natural-language condition. For text/table/questions/answers lanes any text LLM works; for image lanes connect a vision-capable LLM (Claude with vision, GPT-4V, Mistral Vision, Gemini Vision, LLaVA via Ollama).

default.title: Default
default.flow_if_else_llm.question: Does the content match the criterion?

flow_if_else_llm.question.title: Question
flow_if_else_llm.question.description: Natural-language yes/no question evaluated by the connected LLM. The chunk being routed is provided as context. Examples: 'Is this image a screenshot?', 'Is this text in Spanish?', 'Does the image contain a person?'. Be specific — the LLM is instructed to answer with a single word YES or NO; ambiguity in the question costs accuracy.
flow_if_else_llm.question.default: Does the content match the criterion?

flow_if_else_llm.timeout_s.title: Timeout (seconds)
flow_if_else_llm.timeout_s.description: Maximum wall-clock time per evaluation. LLM inference is much slower than sandbox eval, so the default is 30s (vs flow_if_else's 5s). Timeout routes to ELSE (fail-closed).

flow_if_else_llm.branches.title: Branch targets
flow_if_else_llm.branches.description: Maps each branch name to the list of downstream node IDs on that branch. Populated automatically by the canvas when you wire edges from the THEN / ELSE output ports.
